final_prompt.prompt:
Create an original, non-infringing infographic for a technical tutorial on deploying Hermes inside a Docker container and connecting it to GLMCodingPlan, software/DevOps use.

Style/medium: clean flat vector infographic, technical diagram style, Dribbble-quality developer documentation illustration
Composition: vertical single-column layout, top-to-bottom flow with numbered step cards, generous white space between steps
Subject: four clearly labeled sequential steps (①②③④) showing Docker container deployment of Hermes and GLMCodingPlan integration; each step card contains a small icon, a bold step number, a short action title, and a concise command/description block
Scene/backdrop: plain light gray or white background, no decorative environment
Lighting/mood: flat, no shadows, neutral and professional tone
Color palette: Docker blue (#2496ED) as primary accent for container elements, a contrasting teal or green for GLMCodingPlan integration steps, dark charcoal for body text, white card backgrounds
Materials/textures: flat vector, no gradients, crisp edges, monospaced code font for command snippets
Text (verbatim): "1" "2" "3" "4" as step labels; "Docker" on container icons; "Hermes" and "GLMCodingPlan" as component labels
Constraints: step numbers 1-2-3-4 must be visually dominant; arrows or connectors must clearly indicate flow direction; all text must be legible at thumbnail size; layout must be self-contained with no bleed
Avoid: 3D effects, drop shadows, photorealistic textures, decorative gradients, clipart icons, watermarks, overcrowded layouts, ambiguous flow direction
